channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: HUGE amount of street rubbish on wall, 22nd March
item.description:
HUGE amount of street rubbish on wall on Crystal Palace Parade (Lambeth side). See photo of lots cans. This is dreadful and smelling.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 79, Westow Hill, Upper Norwood, City of London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped black bags Selborne Road, 22nd March
item.description:
Flytipped black bags Selborne Road for removal please.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Selborne Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Big and deep pothole, 22nd March
item.description:
Dangerous pothole located in the centre of the road at the junction of Church Hill, Anerley Road and Westow Hill (Crystal Palace), particularly hazardous when turning onto Church Hill. I hit this pothole on my bike today while following a car around the bend. It is positioned in a blind spot and not visible in time to avoid safely. The impact caused an immediate tyre puncture and could easily have resulted in a serious accident. This presents a significant danger to both cyclists and vehicles and requires urgent repair.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 9, Church Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping - Lambeth part of Crystal Palace Parade, 22nd March
item.description:
Fly tipping - Lambeth part of Crystal Palace Parade next to Lambeth waste bin. Lots of discarded wood.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 79, Westow Hill, Upper Norwood, City of London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped cushions and box on Daneville Road, 22nd March
item.description:
Flytipped cushions and box on Daneville Road for removal please. Thank you!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Daneville Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Same vehicle idling every mornong, 22nd March
item.description:
Same vehicle is left idling for prolonged periods of time in the mornings outside property 341 Amhurst Rd, while owner smokes in the vehicle. Exhaust fumes directly affect the young family getting kids ready on bikes for school.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Amhurst Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wheelie bins blocking pavement, 22nd March
item.description:
Wheelie bins from 339 Amhurst Road have repeatedly been left on the pavement for approx 2 months now, making it difficult to pass with buggies/kids/mobility scooters.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Amhurst Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping, 22nd March
item.description:
Rubbish flytipped

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bostall Manorway, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Holes in pavement, 22nd March
item.description:
Possibly caused by riots from a tree being removed Trip hazard Dangerous for blind people Difficult for prams

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cadet Drive, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 4 camper vans/white vans permanently on street, 22nd March
item.description:
4 campervand/white vans are permanently parked on the road and have been for weeks. I think people may be living in them and using the bushes/parkland to relieve themselves and dump rubbish. I don’t feel safe walking past them. There have recently been a number of break ins in the estate with white vans reported as being associated with the break ins. There have also been a number of reports of people seen taking photos of houses and hanging around outside the houses with vans being associated with this also. It makes me feel scared and that the area and my house is not safe.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 8, Rowanwood Avenue, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Assorted rubbish along side green area, 22nd March
item.description:
As per images

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Smalley Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

This pallet has been left here for weeks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Pownall Road, Hackney, London

item.title: A large 12 inch x 6 inch hole at 153A tile kiln lan has developed at the beginning of my driveway, but you can see that there is a gap from there to the path Street. I assume, 22nd March
item.description:
I had already reported this, but hadn’t given enough information and it has exposed further problems since alerting the council of this problem.The street always floods when there is a lot of water in the road outside my house-And there is cracking in the path showing that it is sinking and has now affected my drive.I have checked re-possible water leak on my property, but there is none. I would like someone to have a look because I do think this has occurred from the street and not from the hole that is appeared front of my drive Where there is no leak. There is a big gap under the path which u cannot feel an end to. I have plenty more photos you can see if you wish but it would only allow me to do three .

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tile Kiln Lane, Coldblow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Eastern end bags of rubbish, 22nd March
item.description:
Lots of rubbish on the street, road & bags of rubbish at the Eastern end

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Winchester Avenue, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Manhole cover lose again, 22nd March
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Harrow Manorway, Greater London
